---
title: Theme Overview
description: How the Trellis theme works — ejected components, customization approach, and directory structure.
keywords: [theme, components, ejected, customization]
last_update:
  date: 02/20/2026
  author: Trellis
---

Trellis includes 107 ejected Docusaurus theme components that provide a customized look and improved UX out of the box.

## Ejected vs Wrapped

Docusaurus offers two ways to customize theme components:

- **Wrapping** — import the original component and render it with modifications (keeps upstream updates)
- **Ejecting** — copy the full component source into your project (full control, manual updates)

Trellis uses the **ejected** approach. This gives complete control over every aspect of rendering — from where the last-updated date appears to what icons admonitions use. The trade-off is that upstream Docusaurus theme updates need to be manually reviewed and merged.

## What's Customized

Most of the 107 components are lightly modified or simply ejected for consistency. The significant customizations are:

| Area | What changed | Details |
|------|-------------|---------|
| [Last Updated](/theme/last-updated/) | Date moved to page top | Appears right below the title instead of at the bottom |
| [Heading Anchors](/theme/heading-anchors/) | Copy-to-clipboard | Click the link icon to copy the heading URL |
| [Tabs](/theme/tabs/) | URL sync + pill styling | Tab selection saved to URL query params, pill-style design |
| [Admonitions](/theme/admonitions/) | Custom SVG icons | Each type (note, tip, info, caution, danger) has a unique icon |
| Edit links | Custom pencil icon + "Suggest Edit" | Pencil SVG replaces default; "Suggest Edit" opens a GitHub issue |
| Sidebar | Enhanced styling | Accent colors, hover states, active indicators via CSS tokens |
| Dark mode | Default to dark | Ships with dark mode as default, respects system preference |

## Directory Structure

```
src/theme/
├── Admonition/
│   ├── Icon/          # Note.js, Tip.js, Info.js, Danger.js, Warning.js
│   ├── Layout/        # Admonition container layout
│   ├── Type/          # Type-specific rendering (Caution, Danger, Info, Note, Tip, Warning)
│   ├── Types.js       # Type registry
│   └── index.js       # Main admonition component
├── DocItem/
│   ├── Content/       # ← Main customization: last-updated at top, suggest edit
│   ├── Footer/
│   ├── Layout/
│   ├── Metadata/
│   ├── Paginator/
│   └── TOC/
├── DocRoot/Layout/    # Main page layout + sidebar container
├── DocSidebar/        # Desktop + mobile sidebar
├── DocSidebarItem/    # Category, link, and HTML sidebar items
├── Tabs/              # ← Pill-style tabs with URL sync
├── Details/           # Collapsible sections with brand colors
├── Footer/            # Multi-column footer layout
├── EditThisPage/      # Custom pencil SVG icon
├── NotFound/          # 404 page
├── MDXComponents/     # MDX component overrides
├── MDXContent/
├── AnnouncementBar/
├── DocBreadcrumbs/
├── DocCard/
├── DocCardList/
├── PaginatorNavLink/
├── TabItem/
└── ErrorPageContent.js
```

## Modifying Theme Components

To customize a component, edit it directly in `src/theme/`. Docusaurus automatically resolves components from this directory before falling back to the built-in theme.

:::tip
Before making changes, read the existing component code to understand what it does. Most components import from `@docusaurus/theme-common` or `@docusaurus/theme-common/internal` — check those APIs if you need to understand available hooks and utilities.
:::

:::caution
Since these are ejected components, they won't automatically receive Docusaurus upstream updates. When upgrading Docusaurus, check the [changelog](https://docusaurus.io/changelog) for theme component changes that may need to be manually applied.
:::
